task.title: Replay a failed run
task.shortdesc: Replay a run after fixing a transient issue such as credentials or network instability.

ul.li: You have a failed run_id (for example, run_456).
You have remediated the root cause (for example, updated connector credentials).

context.p: A replay re-executes a previous run using the same job configuration and parameters. This is useful when a run failed due to a transient issue — for example, expired credentials, a brief network outage, or a temporary permissions error — and you have already resolved the root cause. Review the error details to identify the failure before replaying.

step.cmd: Create a replay request for the run.
Check the response for the new run_id.
Monitor the new run to completion.

info.note: The replay creates an entirely new run with its own run_id. The original failed run is not modified.

result.p: The service has created a new run based on the original run configuration.

link.linktext: Run a job and check status
